summary: Website sessions grew 8% month-over-month to 104, driven by organic search traffic growing 40% (10 → 14 sessions). We published two Costa Rica blog posts this month — a travel guide and a corporate incentive trip case study — which are starting to pull in search traffic. LinkedIn posting was paused while we wait on photos and content from the Cancun trip, so impressions dropped significantly. On the wins side, the review QR codes we created for the Cancun trip helped more than double your reviews from ~40 to 92. Next month we're focused on getting Cancun and Costa Rica content live and exploring a LinkedIn ads pilot for the corporate side.

metrics.label: Website Sessions
metrics.note: Up 8% from 96 in February. Organic search up 40%.

metrics.label: LinkedIn Followers
metrics.note: +8 from last month. Steady organic growth without active posting.

metrics.label: LinkedIn Impressions
metrics.note: No new posts this month — waiting on trip photos from Mandy.

metrics.label: Engagement Rate
metrics.note: Still well above the LinkedIn average of 2-3%.

findings.title: Organic Search Traffic Is Growing
findings.description: Google organic sessions went from 10 in February to 14 in March — a 40% increase. Still small numbers, but the two Costa Rica blog posts are starting to index and pull in search traffic. This is the compounding effect of consistent content.

findings.title: Reviews Nearly Doubled After Cancun
findings.description: The QR code review system we set up for the Cancun trip helped drive reviews from approximately 40 to 92 on the TLN site. That social proof is a major asset for future pitches and website credibility.

findings.title: LinkedIn Needs Consistent Fuel
findings.description: Impressions dropped from 1,104 to 92 because no new posts went out this month. The audience engagement rate is strong (6.5%), but LinkedIn's algorithm requires regular posting to maintain reach. Once Cancun and Costa Rica content is ready, we should see a quick rebound.

actions.title: Costa Rica Travel Guide Blog Post
actions.description: Published a full Costa Rica destination travel guide on the website.

actions.title: Costa Rica Corporate Case Study
actions.description: Published "50 People, 3 Days, Zero Burnout" — corporate incentive trip case study showcasing group travel planning.

actions.title: Review QR Codes for Cancun Trip
actions.description: Created QR code review cards for the Cancun spring break trip. Helped drive reviews from ~40 to 92.

actions.title: Content Strategy for April
actions.description: Mapped out next month's content plan including Cancun case study, LinkedIn posts, and Costa Rica page updates.

actions.title: Costa Rica Page Photo Swap
actions.description: Waiting on Dropbox link from Mandy with Costa Rica and Cancun photos to update the page hero image.

actions.title: LinkedIn Content — Cancun & Costa Rica
actions.description: Posts drafted but on hold until trip photos and video content arrive.

nextSteps: Get Cancun and Costa Rica photos from Mandy and update the website pages.
Publish Cancun spring break case study — 347 students, 6 groups, zero-fee model. Great content for LinkedIn and the website.
Resume LinkedIn posting cadence with trip content, aiming for 2-4 posts in April.
Pull best testimonial quotes from TLN reviews for social proof content.
Presenting a LinkedIn Ads proposal at our April 8 meeting — low-budget pilot targeting corporate travel decision-makers.
